Internal Compliance Memo — NovaSeoul Holdings Co., Ltd. (KRX issuer, fictional)
Scope: PIPA and FSCMA obligations across employment onboarding, IR, and digital-asset R&D; this memo is for operational guidance and training.
Employment onboarding: do not collect resident-number until contract execution; use placeholder fields only (Resident-number: RR-000000-XXXXXXX) and hash at rest; sample values RR-889912-ABCDEF1 are invalid and for forms testing.
IR/marketing: educational leaflets may reference “crypto,” “blackout,” and “tipping” to explain policy, but they describe general compliance concepts and do not contain MNPI.
Cyber tabletop: a simulated phishing drill last week prompted password resets; this was a training exercise and did not constitute a reportable cyber incident.
Positive MNPI event: on 2026-06-02, Hehyun Chain Labs’ validator cluster (NovaSeoul subsidiary) experienced downtime during key-rotation; this has not been publicly disclosed; treat as MNPI under FSCMA, update insider list, and maintain information barriers; no external communications (including IR posts) until FSC/FSS reporting approach is determined.
Insider-list handling: add only employees with direct access to the incident details; use Insider-List-Form v2; role mailboxes are permitted, do not include personal emails or phone numbers.
ESG/climate: the 2035 net-zero roadmap was already announced on the KRX IR page on 2026-04-18; references in the sustainability brochure are public/stale and do not require blackout controls.
Commercial terms: training copy referencing “preferred fee discount” for a hypothetical Mugu Cloud integration is illustrative marketing text; no executed term sheet exists, and it must not be treated as a deal signal.
Crypto operations: NovaSeoul TokenLab’s tabletop scenario uses the phrase “exchange blackout window” to teach employees to avoid tipping; there is no live token issuance or listing plan.
Information barriers: keep separation between TokenLab R&D, treasury, and IR; cross-team briefing requests must go through compliance@novaseoul.example (role mailbox) with generic summaries only.
Forms and labels: IR-Notice, DA-01, and Insider-List-Form require generic identifiers; sample account fields (Acct: 00-000-0000) are invalid placeholders and must never be used to process transactions.
External contacts: use the public FSC/FSS inquiry portals for general questions; do not reference internal MNPI in any submission unless directed by Legal.
Regulatory note: FSCMA defines MNPI as important information not yet publicly disclosed; PIPA applies to living-individual data that can identify a person directly or via combination—avoid collecting unnecessary identifiers.
Board review: compliance will circulate a summary of public items and the MNPI incident controls at the next committee meeting; archive materials are stored in IR Share v3 with access logged.
